title: Interior Book Designer / Typesetter (Adobe InDesign) for 6x9 Fantasy Novel
description:
I’m looking for an experienced interior book designer / typesetter to format a long fantasy novel for print.



This is not a cover design job.

This is not an ebook-only formatting job.

This is a print interior layout job for a custom-printed book.



The manuscript is a long-form fantasy novel and will be formatted to 6x9 trim size. I also have a large number of paintings/illustrations that I want placed at the end of chapters throughout the book.



I need someone who understands how to make a book feel like a real, professionally designed object — with clean typography, elegant spacing, strong chapter presentation, and thoughtful image placement.



What I need help with:



Formatting a long fantasy manuscript for 6x9 print

Creating a clean, readable, premium-looking interior

Styling chapter openings and chapter endings

Placing paintings at the end of chapters in a visually pleasing way

Managing page flow and spacing so the book feels intentional

Preparing a print-ready PDF for my printer

Making revisions based on feedback



Requirements:



Strong experience in Adobe InDesign

Experience designing interior book layouts for print

Must understand:

margins

gutters

paragraph styles

master pages

typography

print-ready export settings

image placement for print

Must be able to work with a very long manuscript

Must have experience with books that include illustrations or images

Must be detail-oriented and able to keep formatting consistent across a large document



Nice to have:



Experience with fantasy, fiction, or premium special-edition books

Photoshop experience for preparing illustrations if needed

Experience creating interiors that feel cinematic / elegant / collectible



Deliverables:



Final Adobe InDesign package

Print-ready PDF

Fonts used

Linked image files organized properly

Any supporting exported files needed for printer submission



Important:

Please do not apply if your experience is mostly:



Canva ebooks

KDP low-content books

workbooks/journals only

social media graphics

cover design only



I need someone with real book interior experience.



To apply, please send:



A short note about your experience with print book interiors

2–3 samples of full interior book layouts you’ve designed

At least 1 example of a book interior that includes images/illustrations

Your preferred rate: fixed price or hourly

The words: DARK PULSE LAYOUT at the top of your application so I know you read this



Budget:

Open to proposals, but I am looking for someone experienced and detail-oriented, not the cheapest possible option.

title: CPA/USA Tax Pro accountant
description:
Job Title: CPA / US Tax Accountant (Remote, Full-Time or Part-Time)



We are seeking a highly competent CPA or experienced US Tax Accountant to support a growing group of businesses with accounting, tax compliance, financial organization, and strategic financial oversight. This is not an entry-level bookkeeping role. We are looking for someone with real experience in US accounting and tax work who can operate with accuracy, professionalism, and strong attention to detail.



The right candidate will be comfortable handling financial records, reconciling accounts, preparing reports, supporting tax filings, and helping maintain clean, reliable financials across multiple entities. You should understand US accounting practices, tax requirements, and how to keep a business organized and compliant.



This role is ideal for someone who is dependable, sharp, discreet, and capable of producing high-quality work with minimal supervision.



What You’ll Be Responsible For



Managing day-to-day accounting and financial recordkeeping

Reviewing and reconciling bank accounts, credit cards, loans, and other accounts

Maintaining clean and accurate books

Preparing monthly financial reports and summaries

Assisting with tax preparation, tax compliance, and year-end support

Supporting US federal and state tax filings

Reviewing financial data for errors, missing entries, and inconsistencies

Helping track expenses, allocations, reimbursements, and entity-specific transactions

Supporting payroll, contractor payments, and related reporting if needed

Coordinating with ownership and outside professionals on tax and financial matters

Identifying issues proactively and helping improve financial systems and controls



What We’re Looking For

We are looking for someone who is more than just a data-entry bookkeeper. You should be able to understand the bigger picture, maintain accuracy, and support the financial health of the business at a high level.



You should be:



Experienced in US accounting and tax

Strong in reconciliation, reporting, and financial review

Extremely detail-oriented and organized

Reliable, discreet, and trustworthy

Able to work independently and meet deadlines

Clear and professional in communication

Comfortable working across multiple companies or entities

Proactive in identifying problems before they become bigger issues



Required Qualifications



CPA license or strong experience in US tax and accounting

Proven experience with US-based companies

Strong understanding of US accounting principles and tax processes

Experience with bookkeeping, reconciliations, month-end close, and financial reporting

Ability to prepare for or support tax filings accurately

Strong Excel or Google Sheets skills

Experience using QuickBooks or similar accounting software

Excellent written English

Stable internet connection and reliable remote work setup



Strongly Preferred



Active CPA or EA credential

Experience with multi-entity businesses

Experience with S-Corps, LLCs, owner distributions, reimbursements, and intercompany activity

Familiarity with payroll, 1099s, and contractor reporting

Experience supporting small to mid-sized US businesses

Ability to help improve systems, processes, and reporting clarity



This Role Is Not For



General virtual assistants with light bookkeeping experience

Applicants without real US accounting or tax experience

Candidates who only know basic data entry

People who require constant supervision to stay accurate and on schedule



Why This Role Is Different

This is an opportunity to become a trusted accounting and tax support professional for a growing business group. We are looking for someone long-term who takes pride in precision, understands US financial standards, and wants to contribute at a higher level than routine bookkeeping.



To Apply

Please send the following:



Your resume

A short introduction summarizing your US accounting and tax experience

Your CPA, EA, or other accounting credentials

The accounting software and tax tools you have used

The types of US businesses you have worked with

Your expected monthly salary or hourly rate

Include the word ACCURATE in your application subject line so we know you read the full post

title: Lead Accountant (E-Commerce / Consumer Products)
description:
Location: Remote (Offshore)

Working Hours: Aligned with Central U.S. Time

Compensation: $3,000/month



Overview

 We're a US based pet nutrition and supplement company based in St. Louis, Missouri, founded in 2017 focused on high-quality pet supplements, built around trust, transparency, and product integrity.



This role is not a support function. It is a leadership position within the offshore accounting team.

The Lead Accountant will take full ownership of core accounting operations, oversee a small team, and ensure financial accuracy across the business. This role requires someone who can operate independently, make sound decisions, and maintain a high standard without constant oversight.



Key Responsibilities

>Oversee and supervise Accounts Receivable (AR) and Accounts Payable (AP) processes executed by staff

>Review and approve outgoing payments

>Perform and review bank reconciliations

>Prepare monthly journal entries and ensure accuracy

>Lead the month-end close process and ensure timelines are met

>Prepare audit support and documentation as required

>Review financials and journal entries for completeness and accuracy

>Own revenue reconciliations, including:

1) Retailer-specific rebates

2) Deduction mapping and reconciliation

>Identify gaps, improve processes, and maintain strong internal controls



Team Leadership

>Directly manage and support 2 offshore accounting staff

>Provide guidance, review work, and maintain accountability

>Set standards for accuracy, timeliness, and communication



What Success Looks Like

Month-end close is completed on time, every time, without follow-ups

Financials are clean, accurate, and require minimal revisions

AR and AP workflows run smoothly without bottlenecks

Revenue reconciliations are precise, with clear handling of rebates and deductions

Audit requests are fulfilled quickly with well-organised documentation

The offshore team operates with accountability and consistent output quality

Leadership does not need to chase updates or double-check core work



Ideal Candidate Profile

>Years of experience in accounting, with exposure to ecommerce or consumer products

>Experience leading or mentoring junior accountants

>Strong grasp of revenue reconciliation, including deductions and rebates

>Comfortable owning the month-end close process end-to-end

>Detail-oriented to a fault, with strong analytical thinking

>Direct communicator who can push back when needed

>Must be able to work Central U.S. hours consistently



To Apply: Please send your resume/cv with answers to the ff screening questions below:



1) Do you have previous accounting experience in an e-commerce, retail, or consumer products company?

If yes, please share the company names, industry, and the accounting work you personally handled.



2) Have you worked with U.S.-based clients or companies before?

If yes, please explain the type of work you handled, who you reported to, and how closely you worked with U.S. stakeholders.



3) Have you led, reviewed, or mentored accountants or junior accounting staff? If yes, how many people did you oversee, and what exactly did you review or coach them on?



4) Do you have hands-on experience with revenue reconciliation?

If yes, please explain the types of reconciliations you handled, including any work involving deductions, rebates, chargebacks, or retailer-related adjustments.



5) Are you comfortable working night shift aligned with Central U.S. hours on a full-time basis?



6) Have you personally owned or heavily supported month-end close? If yes, walk us through the parts of the close process you handled directly.



7) What accounting systems and tools have you used most recently?

title: Senior Accountant (Real Estate)
description:
Schedule: Full CST hours 

Salary: $1,800-$2,000+ USD/mon

*basically a Sr Accountant or Controller level individual contributor (not manager)

*can do all hands on work but also prep financial statements, AP/AR, monthly closing, all reports



Position Summary

We are seeking a highly capable and strategic financial operator to serve as a senior-level bookkeeper/controller for our fast-paced, multi-entity real estate enterprise. 



This role supports our real estate brokerage team, investment and development projects, and the personal financial operations of the CEO. The ideal candidate combines technical accounting expertise with strategic insight and the ability to manage complex, multi-entity structures in a lean, high-performance environment.



Key Responsibilities

- Maintain full-cycle bookkeeping for brokerage, investment entities, and project-specific LLCs.

- Oversee job cost accounting, project-level reporting, and GAAP-compliant multi-entity financials.

- Lead monthly closes, consolidate reports, and provide variance analysis.

- Perform weekly/monthly reconciliations of all accounts and ensure books are audit-ready.

- Maintain documentation and prepare reports for CPA coordination and tax filings.

- Ensure accurate 1099 issuance and vendor W-9 compliance.

- Build rolling cash flow forecasts and participate in capital allocation decisions.

- Manage the CEO’s personal finances, including financial tracking, reporting, and investment oversight.

- Support quarterly strategy sessions with data and insight, not just numbers.



Qualifications

- Bachelor's degree in Accounting or Finance; CPA or CMA preferred.

- 5+ years experience in bookkeeping or financial management, ideally in real estate.

- Strong command of US GAAP, project accounting, and inter-entity financial structures.

- Proficiency in QuickBooks, project tracking systems, and financial dashboards.

- Strong analytical thinking, attention to detail, and communication skills.

- Experience managing personal financials for executives a strong plus.



Ideal Candidate Traits

- Strategic thinker who loves both numbers and their meaning.

- Exceptionally organized and accountable.

- Proactive problem-solver who can navigate lean operations.

- Deeply reliable and detail-oriented with a high sense of ownership.

- Comfortable with both the tactical and strategic aspects of finance.



Compensation & Benefits

- Competitive salary based on experience and qualifications.

- Flexible work environment and working remotely.

- Performance-based incentives and potential for expanded leadership role.

title: Support - Community Moderator (Graveyard Shift)
description:
Please apply to this link: 
  



About Us



Wand is building the ultimate game companion — an interaction layer that transforms the gaming experience. Our platform empowers millions of gamers to customize and enhance thousands of games through innovative tooling. What began as a passion project for technical gamers has evolved into a movement, with our best-in-class desktop application becoming essential for players worldwide.



We’re not just building software; we’re crafting the future of gaming, where every player can experience games exactly as they want to. We’re creating an entirely new category in gaming technology that will fundamentally change how people play.



Role Overview



We’re looking for a Support - Community Moderator (US ET Hours) who thrives where gamers hang out and genuinely enjoys engaging with players day to day. This role is highly hands-on: you’ll be active in conversations, keeping communities welcoming and constructive while helping us understand what players are experiencing in real time.



Beyond moderation, you’ll act as a community pulse-checker — spotting trends, recurring questions, sentiment shifts, and emerging issues across our channels. You’ll surface insights early, help players feel heard, and ensure the right teams have visibility into what’s happening on the ground.



If you enjoy engaging with gaming communities, have strong 
 moderation experience, and like connecting patterns in conversations to real product or support insights, this role is for you.



Key Responsibilities

Community Engagement

- Be an active and approachable presence across Wand’s community spaces:

- Engage with players, answer questions, and guide users to the right resources

- Encourage positive discussion and help reinforce a healthy community culture

- Maintain an on-brand voice while adapting tone and style per platform

- Collect player feedback and surface common questions or concerns internally



Primary channels:

title: Expert Video Editor - Davinci
description:
** PLEASE READ ALL THE INSTRUCTIONS & ONLY APPLY IF YOU HAVE EXTENSIVE VIDEO EDITING EXPERIENCE**



Are you a talented video editor with a passion for creating captivating, fast-paced social media videos? I’m looking for a skilled professional to finalize high-quality 1-2 minute videos for my social media platforms.



About the Role



I need a highly dependable, truly exceptional video editor who can learn to replicate my video editing style. This role involves quick turn around times and large amounts of footage. I need someone passionate about storytelling and who has a great eye for selecting the best 
nts out of hundreds (sometimes thousands) of clips and arranging them in a way that goes along with a voiceover in the most compelling and powerful way possible.



You'll be assisting my team of video editors by stepping in during the second half of the video creation process, working with raw, already chopped footage, voiceovers, and initial edits I’ve prepared to produce videos ready for another tea
mber to color grade. Each project will involve 20 minutes to 2 hours of pre-chopped footage, which you’ll sift through to select the best clips and create a 1–2 1/2 minute video that tells a compelling story. 



Our team includes a part time video color-grader and captioner so most likely you will not be color grading or captioning the final videos. You will be our expert clip arranger who can arrange the footage according to my voiceover in a compelling way, using only the very best pieces of footage available.



This is a per project roll that pays $35 USD a video, with 3-5 videos a week on average. Our video output fluctuates, so some weeks there may be more work available for you if you'd like it. 



My ideal video editor must be confident that they can create videos in my signature style:



Example 1: 

Example 2: 

Example 3: 





Key Responsibilities



- Edit and arrange video clips to align with pre-recorded voiceovers.



- Review and narrow down footage to select the most compelling clips.



- Craft engaging and visually appealing videos that flow naturally and tell a cohesive story.



- Incorporate relevant visuals such as screen recordings, maps, and on-screen text when necessary.



- Ensure videos feature dynamic intros/hooks to capture viewers' attention and match with video intros I've created in the past.



- Collaborate via DaVinci for seamless project sharing and updates.





What I’m Looking For



- Proven Expertise: Advanced video editing skills with a portfolio of engaging short-form content.



- Proficiency in DaVinci 



- Attention to Detail: Ability to identify and highlight the best 
nts in raw footage.



- Storytelling Skills: Strong sense of pacing and narrative structure.



- Social Media Savvy: Familiarity with the style and trends of platforms like TikTok and Instagram.



- An editor who can create videos in my signature style.





- What I Provide



- Raw footage and voiceovers for each project.



- Clear direction and collaboration throughout the editing process.



- Flexibility to foster creativity and innovation in the editing process.





How to Apply:



1. Change the subject line to how many years experience you have editing videos



2. (IMPORTANT) Provide links to 3-5 specific videos you've edited that showcase your abilities to create videos like my example videos. Please do not give any examples of talking-head style videos. 



3. Please send your portfolio



4. Answer the following questions:



- Kindly illustrate how the linked videos you've provided demonstrate your suitability for the role and why you would excel in editing travel and vlog videos.



- What software do you use to edit videos? And are you willing to use Davinci for an easier collaboration process?



- Do you have any other experience that will make you a perfect fit for the job?



- Do you feel confident that you can edit 3-5 high quality videos each week, for $35 USD a video?



- Are you open to doing a paid test task? 



Thank you for your time, and I look forward to chatting with you!

title: Senior Risk & Compliance Consultant (Fintech & Crypto)
description:
We need an expert who bridges the gap between Web2 Traditional Finance (Fiat Rails) and Web3 On-chain Compliance.

Your core mission is not just to be a "blocker," but to "design safe mechanisms that enable business velocity." You will ensure we pass the rigorous onboarding requirements of our banking partners (e.g., Cybrid's AML program review) while managing the operational risks of a high-volume payment platform.



Key Responsibilities

Compliance Program Build-out

Draft BSA/AML Policies from Scratch: Create internal compliance policy documents (CIP, AML, OFAC) that meet US banking standards. This is a prerequisite for going live with partners like Cybrid.

Define KYC/KYB Standards: Design tiered identity verification workflows for our dual-track business (B2B Agency + B2C Palau ID). Define limits and requirements for Tier 1 vs. Tier 2 users.

Regulatory Strategy: Assess licensing requirements (MTL/MSB/MiCA) for US, Canada, and EU markets, providing a strategic roadmap for the next 12-24 months.

Operational Risk & Fraud Prevention

ACH Risk Management: specifically for ACH Pull scenarios. Design logic to mitigate NSF (Insufficient Funds) and Unauthorized Returns (e.g., balance checks via Plaid, settlement holding periods).

On-Chain Risk Integration: Configure on-chain analysis tools (e.g., GoPlus, Chainalysis, Elliptic) to define blocking rules for high-risk wallet addresses.

Transaction Monitoring: Set up velocity limits and alert rules for suspicious activities; handle Suspicious Activity Reports (SARs) if necessary.

Partner Enablement

Due Diligence (DD): Represent the company in handling compliance inquiries from upstream banking partners and infrastructure providers (Cybrid, Hex Trust), assisting in completing complex DD questionnaires.

Vendor Assessment: Evaluate the compliance robustness of new payment rails (e.g., European SEPA, Asian local payouts).



Requirements

Experience: 5+ years of risk/compliance experience in Fintech or Crypto Exchanges. Must have hands-on experience with US Banking Rails (ACH/Wire).

Expertise:

Deep knowledge of FinCEN, OFAC sanctions, and AML workflows.

Strong understanding of the "FBO Account" model and the legal boundaries of a "Money Transmitter."

Understanding of stablecoin (USDC) minting and burning flows.

Tool Stack: Familiarity with industry-standard tools like Persona (Identity), Plaid (Bank Auth), and Chainalysis/TRM Labs (Crypto AML).

Mindset: Ability to balance "Compliance Requirements" with "User Experience." We don't need a bureaucrat; we need a builder who offers solutions.



Bonus Points

Certified Anti-Money Laundering Specialist (CAMS) certification.

Experience helping startups successfully onboard with Zero Hash, Cybrid, Circle, or Stripe.

Knowledge of RNS.ID (Palau Digital Residency) or similar sovereign identity projects.

Familiarity with India TDS tax regulations or Philippines cross-border remittance laws.

title: DevOps Engineer
description:
Job Description:



As a DevOps Engineer at the Republic of Palau's Digital Residency Program (RNS.ID), you will be a crucial part of our team, responsible for managing and optimizing our cloud infrastructure. Your expertise in AWS, GCP, and Terraform will be pivotal in ensuring the reliability, scalability, and security of our Web3 ID platform.



Key Qualifications:



    Proficiency in Amazon Web Services (AWS) and Google Cloud Platform (GCP).



    Strong expertise in Terraform for infrastructure as code.



    Proven experience in maintaining and optimizing cloud-based infrastructure.



    Excellent problem-solving and troubleshooting skills.



    Ability to work collaboratively in a dynamic environment.



    Familiarity with DevOps best practices.



Responsibilities:



-Infrastructure Management: Design, deploy, and maintain AWS and GCP infrastructure to support the Web3 ID platform. Utilize Terraform for infrastructure provisioning and configuration. Monitor and optimize cloud resources for performance and cost-efficiency.



-Automation and CI/CD: Develop and maintain automation scripts and pipelines for continuous integration and continuous delivery (CI/CD). Implement containerization and orchestration technologies (e.g., Docker, Kubernetes) to streamline deployment processes.



-Security and Compliance: Collaborate with the security team to implement and maintain security best practices. Conduct regular security assessments and ensure compliance with relevant regulations and standards.



-Monitoring and I
 Response: Set up and manage monitoring and alerting systems to proactively identify and address issues. Investigate and resolve infrastructure-related i
, conducting root cause analysis.



-Documentation: Create and update infrastructure documentation and procedures. Document i
 reports and resolutions for knowledge sharing.



Qualifications:



-Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, or related field (or equivalent   work experience).

-Minimum of 3 years of experience as a DevOps Engineer, Cloud Engineer, or similar role.

-AWS and GCP certifications are a plus.

-Strong understanding of cloud architecture and best practices.

-Familiarity with Web3 and blockchain technologies is advantageous.



Join RNS.ID and contribute to our mission of revolutionizing digital identity through Web3 technology.

title: Appointment Setters - $50 per Zoom Call
description:
Talented Appointment Setters - Earn $200 to $500 per day 



I’m expanding a Google Maps lead-generation service and need confident phone pros to lock in owner-attended Zoom calls with local Australian businesses. I’ll supply a proven script, talk tracks, rebuttals and a Calendar link; you focus purely on dialing straight from Google Map searches, qualifying and scheduling.



Who you’ll be calling

Think independent operators and small chains across Healthcare (physios, dentists, chiros), Home & Commercial services (cleaners, landscapers, gutter crews), Trades (builders, sparkies, plumbers, glaziers), plus Automotive repairers and Professional firms such as lawyers or accountants. Call directly from Google Map searches, simple.



Must have your own voip phone system and reliable internet to call Australian businesses.



What you’re offering them

A free Google Maps visibility audit that pinpoints why they aren’t ranking in the local pack, plus easy DIY fixes or a paid option with my team. Your goal is a 15-minute Zoom session where I or another strategist deliver that audit live.



Pay structure

• $50 AUD for every Zoo
eting that the prospect attends.

• Typical setters average 100+ completed calls daily, so $200–$400 AUD is realistic.

• Commissions are tallied and paid every Thursday based on the previous week's results.



Your toolkit

• Ready-to-use script & objection flow

• Calender access for seamless booking

• Training video at 
 for product context



Deliverables (acceptance criteria)

1. Prospect fits target industries and is a decision maker.

2. Zoom slot booked in Calendar with correct time zone.

3. Prospect attends the call (status automatically confirmed in Zoom/CRM).

4. Brief call note entered in spreadsheet



If you’re persuasive on the phone, disciplined with follow-up and comfortable using a script, let’s get you dialing within 24 hours. Apply with a short note on your outbound experience and what phone dialer you have to call from.

title: Controller (US e-commerce)
description:
Title: Controller



Looking for a disciplined financial owner who brings calm to complexity 



The Controller role at FS is for someone who enjoys applying knowledge, logic, and structure to create reliable financial systems. This is not a CFO or advisory role. It is a role for a financial professional who takes ownership of execution and finds satisfaction in predictable, trusted outcomes. 



When this role is done well, leadership stops double-checking numbers, inventory decisions are disciplined, and finance becomes boring in the best possible way. 



If you prefer facts over narratives, systems over heroics, and clarity over urgency, this role is built for you.



The mission 

Your mission is to own the financial operating system. You ensure financials are accurate, 

timely, and decision-ready, with clear cash visibility and enforced controls, without requiring 

constant leadership involvement. 



What you will do 

? Own monthly close and deliver accurate financials on a consistent schedule. 

? Ensure all accounts are reconciled and audit-ready. 

? Maintain clear tracking of owner expenses. 

? Prepare board-ready financial summaries with variances and cash implications. 

? Build and maintain a rolling 13-week cash flow forecast. 

? Surface liquidity risks and tradeoffs early. 

? Own inventory finance and open-to-buy controls. 

? Review open-to-buy monthly with Supply Chain and approve or block purchasing. 

? Design and enforce financial controls and approval workflows. 

? Own AR and AP systems and performance. 

? Direct and review the work of the Accountant. 

? Improve AR procedures to support cash timing. 

? Coordinate sales tax obligations and R&D tax tracking. 

? Audit key vendor invoices and charges as needed. 



What success looks like 

? Financials are delivered on time, every month. 

? Cash visibility is clear, current, and trusted. 

? Inventory decisions reflect cash discipline. 

? Controls prevent errors and surprises. 

? Finance operates calmly and predictably. 

? Leadership trusts the numbers without rechecking. 



This role is for you if 

? You enjoy structured, analytical work. 

? You prefer enforcing standards over negotiating them. 

? You like applying proven methods rather than inventing new ones. 

? You are comfortable owning deadlines and saying no when needed. 

? You communicate in a direct, factual way. 



This role is not for you if 

? You want a strategy-first or advisory role. 

? You dislike routine or repetition. 

? You prefer autonomy without guardrails. 

? You thrive on urgency, ambiguity, or constant change.



To Apply: send cv/resume and answers to the ff questions below:

1) Do you have 5+ years of hands-on accounting/controller experience owning the monthly close process?

If yes, briefly explain what you personally owned end-to-end.



2) Have you worked in a product-based business such as e-commerce, retail, wholesale, inventory-heavy, or consumer goods? If yes, name the company/industry and describe the finance work you handled.



3) Do you have experience with inventory finance, open-to-buy planning, or approving/blocking purchasing decisions based on cash and inventory position?



4) Have you led, reviewed, or managed the work of an Accountant or junior accounting staff? If yes, how many people did you oversee, and what work did you review?



5) Have you worked with U.S. accounting operations, including areas such as sales tax, accruals, month-end close, board reporting, or cash planning? If yes, explain which areas you handled.



6) Are you fully available to work CST hours for this role?

title: Executive Assistant
description:

Full Time | Remote | US Hours

If you’re discreet, proactive, and able to manage both professional and personal priorities without constant direction, this role might be the right fit for you.



What You Will Do

• Act as a trusted gatekeeper with internal teams and external contacts

• Coordinate complex travel plans, including last-minute changes

• Track deadlines, commitments, and follow-ups to ensure nothing is missed

• Support personal logistics such as appointments, reminders, and important dates

• Assist with projects, publishing initiatives, and one-off executive priorities

• Coordinate across departments, including operations, finance, and creative teams



Who You Are

• 7+ years of experience supporting senior executives, founders, or public-facing leaders

• Highly professional, discreet, and calm under pressure

• Strong judgment with the confidence to push back diplomatically when needed

• Proactive, solutions-oriented, and able to anticipate needs

• Comfortable managing both personal and professional domains seamlessly

• Highly responsive and dependable, with flexibility outside standard business hours

• Experienced working remotely and staying aligned across time zones



To apply, send your resume along with answers to the questions below. To be considered, please ensure all questions are answered in full.

1. Walk me through a day where everything felt urgent. How did you d
 what actually got the CEO’s attention first?

2. How do you d
 when to act independently versus when to loop the CEO in?

3. Tell me about a time you had to push back on someone requesting the CEO’s time. What did you say and why?

4. What information do you automatically treat as non-shareable, even internally? Can you give an example?

5. Describe a last-minute change — travel, schedule, or event — and what you did first.

title: E-commerce Marketing Coordinator (Full-time)
description:
Location: Remote

Department: Marketing / E-commerce

Reports to: Marketing Manager

Schedule: Full CST hours 



Why This Role Exists

This role exists to own day-to-day e-commerce execution so launches, promotions, and PDP updates don’t bottleneck at the Marketing Manager level.

The E-commerce Marketing Associate consolidates Shopify execution, launch coordination, and marketing operations into a single accountable role. As launch volume increases (3–4/month), this role improves speed, accuracy, and execution quality across PDPs, promotions, and site updates.

You’ll act as the execution right-hand to the Marketing Manager — owning backend setup, QA, CRO execution, and cross-functional coordination, while surfacing risks early and keeping work moving forward.



Role Scorecard & Outcomes

E-commerce Execution & Site Ownership

Shopify backend is clean, accurate, and fully maintained. PDPs, collections, promotions, and homepage updates are executed correctly and on time with minimal supervision.

Launch Readiness & QA

All launches are fully set up, QA’d, and executed without last-minute issues or escalations.

Conversion & CRO Support

PDP and site improvements are consistently implemented, contributing to improved CVR, engagement, or UX clarity over baseline.

Inventory Awareness & Readiness

Inventory status is clearly understood from a marketing perspective. OOS risks, restocks, and back-in-stock opportunities are proactively flagged and supported through site updates and campaign readiness.

Operational Reliability

Reporting, inventory visibility, and promo-related site updates are accurate, timely, and dependable.

Cross-Functional Coordination

Designers, copywriters, inventory, and agencies receive clear briefs, timelines, and follow-ups. Work progresses without repeated intervention from the Marketing Manager.



Key Responsibilities

E-commerce & Website Execution

Build and manage PDPs in the Shopify backend (SKUs, variants, pricing, bundles, collections).

Implement PDP copy (from Copywriter) and visual assets (from Designer).

Execute homepage, collection, and promotional site updates.

Maintain site hygiene: broken links, formatting issues, outdated content, and UX friction.

QA the site prior to launches to ensure accuracy, functionality, and readiness.

Integrate SEO content into the website.

Conversion Rate Optimization (CRO)

Support CRO initiatives including layout adjustments, upsells, trust elements, and merchandising improvements.

Execute approved A/B tests and experiments; document changes and results.

Product Launch Execution

Coordinate product launches and promotions based on the marketing calendar.

Own backend launch setup end-to-end (products, pricing, promos, collections, inventory sync).

Conduct basic competitor pricing analysis and margin checks when required.

Support post-launch performance summaries and insights.

Marketing Operations

Maintain daily, weekly, and monthly scorecards.

Monitor inventory levels relevant to marketing execution (OOS, low stock, restocks, incoming inventory).

Partner with Inventory and Operations teams to stay aligned on availability, timing, and constraints.

Support back-in-stock, relaunch, and inventory-driven campaigns through site and launch readiness.

Coordinate with internal teams and external agencies to keep timelines on track.

Flag execution risks, blockers, or dependencies early — with proposed solutions.



Ways of Working & Mindset

Ownership mindset: Takes responsibility for outcomes, not just tasks.

Bias toward action: Moves work forward with imperfect information.

Commercially aware: Understands how inventory, timing, and execution impact revenue.

Detail-obsessed but pragmatic: High accuracy without paralysis.

Solution-oriented: Comes with options, not just problems.

Calm under pressure: Handles launch changes without noise.

Right-hand mentality: Thinks one step ahead to reduce load on the Marketing Manager.





Core Competencies

Strong Shopify and e-commerce backend fluency.

High attention to detail and execution accuracy.

CRO and merchandising fundamentals.

Inventory awareness for e-commerce and lifecycle marketing.

Clear written communication and coordination skills.

Comfort working with performance data and reporting.



Candidate Must-Haves

Proven experience executing e-commerce updates in Shopify or similar platforms.

Strong analytical literacy using GA4, Shopify Analytics, and/or heat map tools (Hotjar, Clarity).

Ability to manage multiple execution tasks with speed and accuracy.

Strong problem-solving instincts and independent judgment.

Excellent QA discipline, especially around launches, pricing, promos, and inventory-driven updates.



Nice-to-Haves

Experience in DTC or high-SKU e-commerce environments with frequent launches.

Exposure to CRO frameworks beyond basic A/B testing.

Experience coordinating with paid media, lifecycle, or
 teams.

Familiarity with strength training, home gym equipment, or enthusiast-driven brands.



PLEASE READ CAREFULLY: To apply send your CV/resume with answers to the screening questions below: 

You'll own Shopify execution so launches, promos, and site updates happen fast and correctly — without the Marketing Manager babysitting every detail. That means you'll work with teams here to ensure everything is accurate.



1) Tell me about a Shopify/E-commerce launch (ex. major site update, product launch, promo launch etc.).What was your day-by-day execution plan, who are the teams involved, how do you ensure all teams are on top of things? Please break them down and share situations where you had to step in. 



2) Before launching something high-impact, what is your personal QA checklist? What are 5 things that commonly break that others forget to check?



3) Tell me about a time you had to set something up in a system where accuracy was critical before it went live (e.g., product setup, pricing update, bulk edits, system configuration).

title: Analytics Virtual Assistant — Ministry of Growth
description:
Analytics Virtual Assistant — Ministry of Growth

$700–$1,000/month | Full-time | Remote | Philippines-based | HIRING ASAP



? CEO interview on April 3 (Friday). No Loom video = no interview. Apply now.



ABOUT MINISTRY OF GROWTH

Ministry of Growth is not an agency. Not a consultancy. Not another vendor that charges a retainer, sends a monthly report, and moves on. We are an embedded growth partner. We go inside creator businesses, build the revenue engine, and operate it alongside them. Our model is simple: we only win when they win. Revenue share means our incentives are locked to your results. Always.



THE ROLE

You'll be our data person. Every day, you pull performance data from ad accounts and dashboards. Every week, you update all client dashboards and prepare the reports our team needs to make decisions. You're the reason our Growth Operators can open a dashboard on Monday morning and know exactly how every client is doing.



YOUR WEEKLY RHYTHM



DAILY

- Pull spend, impressions, clicks, leads, CPL, cost per booked call fro
ta & Google

- Update the daily tracker

- Flag any metric that moves 20%+ — alert in Slack immediately



WEEKLY

- Update per-client and portfolio-level dashboards every Friday

- Compile Monday scorecard by 8am — green/yellow/red per client, 5-min founder review

- Fulfill ad-hoc data requests from Growth Operators same day



MONTHLY

- Compile trend analysis across all clients — what improved, what declined, patterns (AI assists with this)

- Present findings in a clean, shareable report format



MUST-HAVES

- Fast and accurate in Google Sheets / Excel — pivot tables, formulas, charts

- Experience pulling data fro
ta Ads Manager or Google Ads (or fast learner)

- Available during European business hours (or significant overlap)

- Strong written English

- Extremely accurate — one wrong number leads to a wrong decision

- Consistent — same quality every single day, not just when you feel like it

- Fast — when someone needs data, they need it in hours, not days

- Proactive — you flag problems before anyone has to ask



NICE TO HAVE

- Agency Analytics, Google Data Studio, or similar dashboard tools

- Marketing agency background

- Knows CPL, ROAS, CTR, close rate

- Comfortable using AI for analysis and report drafting



WHAT WE OFFER

- $700–$1,000/month — above market for PH, paid consistently

- Long-term role — you'll grow with us from 3 clients to 10+

- Clear daily and weekly routines — you'll always know what to do

- Small team where your accuracy directly impacts business decisions

- Path to a senior analytics role as we scale



HOW TO APPLY — FAST AND SIMPLE

Graphic Designer (Marketing & Ad Creatives) – Long-Term Opportunity



We’re looking for a graphic designer who can create clean, modern, and effective visuals for digital marketing.



Our team works with businesses across the U.S. and Canada, building marketing systems that include ads, funnels, and automation. Design plays a key role in how these systems perform, and we’re looking for someone who understands both creativity and clarity.



This is a long-term opportunity for someone who wants consistent work, growth, and to be part of a fast-moving team.



What You’ll Be Doing:

- Design ad creatives for platforms like
 and Instagram

- Create visuals for landing pages and funnels

- Produce clean, modern graphics aligned with brand and messaging

- Develop multiple variations of designs for testing and performance

- Support with banners, thumbnails, and other digital assets



You’ll work closely with our marketing and video teams to bring ideas to life visually.



What We’re Looking For:

- Strong design skills with a clean, modern style

- Experience creating ad creatives for digital marketing campaigns

- Good sense of layout, hierarchy, and visual clarity

- Strong attention to detail

- Ability to follow direction and also contribute ideas

- Reliable, efficient, and able to meet deadlines

- Good English communication

- Experience designing ad creatives that are meant to convert

- Understanding of marketing or performance-based design

- Ability to follow brand guidelines and maintain consistency across designs



Bonus to Have:

- Experience working with video editors or marketing teams

- Basic motion or animation skills



Important:

This is not a beginner role.

We are looking for someone who is comfortable working independently and can produce high-quality work without step-by-step guidance.

You should understand that design is not just about visuals — it’s about creating assets that support performance and results



Work Structure & Growth Path:

- Initial work will start with small tasks and project-based assignments (gig-based)

- This allows both sides to evaluate fit and working style

- Strong performance will quickly move into a part-time role (approximately 20 hours per week)

- From there, the role can grow into a full-time position (approximately 40 hours per week)



We are looking for someone interested in a long-term role — not short-term work.



Compensation:

- Starting benchmark: $5 USD per hour

What You Will Do:

Native Formatting: Apply native-looking text overlays and captions that blend seamlessly into social feeds.



What We Do NOT Want (Please read carefully):



Do NOT apply if you only edit cinematic travel vlogs. We are a Direct Response brand. Every second must be designed to convert.

Experience prompting AI video/image generators to match a specific brand aesthetic.

day check-ins

title: Experience Email Marketer
description:
ABOUT DISPENZA

Dispenza has quickly become one of the largest marketing agencies in the rapidly-growing emerging market of cannabis as it quickly legalizes state by state in the Americas. As the market matures, we are on a mission to help change how the market operates, leaving the more questionable activities done by current market leaders and emerge as the first marketing agency and platform that values our customers while simultaneously delivering business-changing results to our clients.



JOB OVERVIEW

We’re looking for an experience
 Marketer to support ongoing campaign execution, lifecycle marketing, and retention strategy. This role focuses on building and optimizing
 campaigns, automations, SMS, and Wallet/ePass messaging to drive engagement, retention, and revenue.



KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

- Plan, build, and execute
 marketing campaigns, including one-off broadcasts and automated lifecycle flows

- Develop and manage automations such as welcome series, abandoned cart, re-engagement, and retention sequences

- Segment audiences and implement targeted messaging strategies based on customer behavior and lifecycle stage

- Design and run A/B tests on subject lines, content, timing, and campaign structure to improve performance

- Monitor, analyze, and report on campaign performance (open rates, CTR, conversions, and revenue attribution)

- Translate performance data into actionable insights and optimization strategies

- Create clear, concise performance reports with recommendations for continuous improvement

- Support Wallet/ePass and SMS campaign initiatives as part of a broader retention strategy

- Ensure all campaigns are aligned with brand voice, compliance standards, and business goals



TEAM COLLABORATION

- Work closely with project managers, designers, and stakeholders to execute campaigns efficiently

- Participate in team reviews to evaluate performance and identify opportunities for improvement

- Communicate updates proactively and flag blockers, risks, or optimization opportunities early



REQUIRED SKILLS & EXPERIENCE

- 3–5 years of experience in
 marketing

- Hands-on experience with platforms such as Klaviyo, HubSpot, Mailchimp, or Salesforce

- Proven track record managing high-volume
 lists and driving measurable revenue impact

- Strong understanding of segmentation, lifecycle marketing, and customer journey mapping

- Experience building and optimizing high-performing campaigns and automations

- Analytical mindset with the ability to interpret data and translate it into actionable strategies

- Highly organized, detail-oriented, and reliable

- Strong communication skills with the ability to provide clear updates and ask thoughtful questions



DESIRED SKILLS & EXPERIENCE

- Experience with retention marketing in eCommerce or DTC brands

- Familiarity with SMS and/or Wallet (ePass) marketing campaigns

- Basic design awareness and experience working with templates or tools like Figma or Canva

- Experience in regulated industries (e.g., cannabis is a plus, but not required)



COMPENSATION

$500-$900 starting depending on experience.



SCHEDULE

- 8 hour shift, Monday to Friday, 7:00am to 4:00pm Philippine Time.

- Minimal overtime during weekdays and weekends will be expected as part of salary



Dispenza is proud to be an equal opportunity employer committed to a diverse and inclusive work environment. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, national origin, wordpressage, marital status, disability, veteran status or any other basis protected by law.



HOW TO APPLY

Please fill out the following form in order for your application to be considered: 



Good luck!

title: Experienced Associate Brand Manager to support 12+ Amazon brands for growth, execution, drive performance and operation
description:
Role Overview



We're hiring 2 Associate Brand Manager who will work directly under the Senior Brand Manager to execute day-to-day brand operations. This is a hands-on role focused on the “legwork” that drives performance: optimizing listings, managing keyword research, coordinating creative projects, and pulling actionable data.



If the Senior Brand Manager sets the playbook, the Associate Brand Manager runs the plays — ensuring everything gets executed on time, correctly, and at a high level.



Key Responsibilities

1. Listing Management & Optimization

Create, update, and fully optimize Amazon product listings (titles, bullets, descriptions, images, videos, A+ content).

Conduct keyword research and competitor analysis to improve rankings and visibility.

Ensure all listings remain Rufus and Cosmos compliant with Amazon’s latest requirements.

Test content variations (A/B testing) to improve CTR and conversions.



2. Data Research & Analysis

Pull performance data from Amazon Seller Central and external tools (Data Dive, Helium 10, Jungle Scout).

Identify trends, keyword shifts, and competitor activity.

Provide actionable recommendations to the Senior Brand Manager to improve conversions, CTR, and sales velocity.



3. Cross-Team Collaboration

Work closely with the graphic design team to request, coordinate, and deliver images, infographics, and A+ creatives.

Partner with the marketing team to support product launches, promotions, and review-generation initiatives.

Ensure all projects are aligned with deadlines, strategy, and brand guidelines.



4. Conversion-Focused Execution

Continuously update and refine listings to improve conversion rates and customer engagement.

Maintain Amazon Brand Stores, A+ content, and ad creatives for consistency and performance.

Assist in preparing assets for influencer campaigns and external marketing pushes.



5. Project & Compliance Support

Own the administrative “heavy lifting” of flat files, bulk uploads, and catalog troubleshooting.

Flag compliance issues, unauthorized sellers, or listing errors for quick resolution.

Support market expansion efforts by preparing research and draft listings for new product opportunities.



Skills & Qualifications



2+ years experience in Amazon FBA operations, brand management, or digital marketing.

Proficient in Amazon Seller Central, flat files, listing creation, and troubleshooting.

Strong skills in SEO, keyword research, and listing optimization.

Data-driven mindset; confident in Google Sheets and reporting.

Strong communication skills to work cross-functionally with design, marketing, and operations teams.

Highly organized, detail-oriented, and capable of managing multiple deadlines.



What Success Looks Like

Every listing across assigned brands is fully optimized, compliant, and performing.

Senior Brand Manager receives clear, accurate reports and recommendations.

Creative assets are delivered on time because you coordinated smoothly with design.



Product launches happen seamlessly with marketing and review strategies in place.

Conversions and rankings consistently improve due to your execution. If you feel you are the right fit, please respond to the job with the title " The Best Supporting Branding Manager Out There!"



Please respond directly to :

----------



Kind Regards,

Tam Ma

Co Founder

Prodigy Media

title: Real Estate Portfolio Finance Manager
description:
*KINDLY READ THE JOB DESCRIPTION & THE APPLICATION INSTRUCTIONS CAREFULLY.*



Who We Are:



Tracing our history back to 1920, EJF Real Estate Services has been a family-owned and family-operated business for over 30 years, specializing in community association management and real estate sales across the Washington, DC metropolitan area. Today, we are the largest DC-based property management firm, proudly serving hundreds of clients throughout the DMV. Our extensive market knowledge, hands-on industry experience, and best-in-class customer service help communities thrive. 



What You'll Do:



As a Finance Manager, you’ll oversee the financial reporting and analysis for a portfolio of community associations. This is a client-facing, oversight role — not transactional bookkeeping. You’ll ensure accurate monthly financials, guide Boards of Directors in understanding their financial position, and partner with internal teams to resolve issues. This position is ideal for an experienced accounting professional who thrives in a dynamic, client-service environment.



Requirements:



Ensure monthly financial reports accurately reflect the association’s financial position.

Review general ledgers, statements of revenue/expenses, and balance sheets to resolve issues and correct errors.

Serve as the primary financial resource for both internal tea
mbers and external clients (board members and association representatives).

Oversee and ensure completion of administrative tasks, including tax payments, end-of-year tax returns, and government filings.

Support external audits for associations within the portfolio.

Review and monitor banking and investment activity.

Analyze and review loans, special assessments, and other financial obligations as required.

Provide long-term financial planning guidance for capital projects.

Attend weekly tea
etings, set goals, and complete assigned tasks on time.

Perform additional duties as assigned.



Qualifications:



Bachelor’s degree in Accounting, Finance, or related field (or equivalent experience).

3+ years of accounting, financial experience in property management (HOA, condo, coop) is required.

Experience working with Vantaca property management software is required.

Strong knowledge of GAAP and financial reporting.

Excellent analytical, problem-solving, and organizational skills.

Strong interpersonal and communication skills with the ability to present financial information clearly to non-financial stakeholders.

Must be able to analyze data issues and execute on solutions.

Proficiency in accounting software and Microsoft Excel.



Working Hours: 9:00 am – 5:00 pm EST; Monday – Friday (US time zones)



Benefits: HMO and PTO after 90 days.



How to apply:

title: Transaction Coordinator - Loan Mortgage Underwriter
description:
Funding is a U.S.-based private money lending company that provides short-term, asset-based loans to real estate investors. We focus on first- and second-position loans, bridge funding, gap funding, and creative real estate transactions.



We value clean execution, capital protection, and professional communication.



Role Overview



We are hiring a Loan Processor/ Jr. Underwriter to support real estate deals from intake through funding. This role is the operational backbone of our lending business and works directly with borrowers, brokers, and internal leadership.



This is not a bank mortgage role. You will support private money deals using CRM pipelines and internal workflows.



Key Responsibilities



Manage real estate deals inside the CRM.

Collect, organize, and review borrower and property documents.

Assist with preliminary deal review (LTV, ARV, exit strategy – no approvals)

Prepare deals for internal review and decision-making

Communicate with borrowers and brokers via
, phone, and chat

Request missing documents and follow up to keep deals moving

Coordinate with title companies and insurance providers for closings



Track conditions, timelines, and closing tasks

Maintain clean CRM records, notes, and document uploads

Assist with light lead follow-up and deal intake

Important Authority Boundaries







Requirements

2+ years experience in real estate transactions, loan processing, or lending support

Strong attention to detail and organization

Excellent written and spoken English

Comfortable communicating with U.S.-based clients

Ability to manage multiple deals at once

Experience using CRMs and pipeline-based systems



Nice to Have (Not Required):

Private money or hard money lending experience

Familiarity with real estate investing terminology

GoHighLevel or similar CRM experience



What Success Looks Like

Clean, accurate CRM records

Deals move smoothly with minimal rework

Borrowers feel informed and supported

Leadership does not need to fix or chase files



How to Apply

Please apply with:

Please submit your resume along with a brief introduction explaining your relevant experience and why you’re an excellent fit for this role. Include the word “Lending" in your application message so we know you read this fully.

title: Service Coordinator - Maintenance Specialist
description:
Department:

SJA Pros



Reports To:

Operations Coordinator 



Role Type:

Full-Time, Remote



The Service Coordinator is responsible for driving the daily movement of work orders within assigned trades (such as plumbing, HVAC, remediation, or carpentry). This role ensures rapid assignment, efficient scheduling, and consistent progress across all active jobs.



Acting as the central point of coordination between SJA Pros, subcontractors, and the Maintenance Services Team, the Service Coordinator maintains clear communication, accountability, and 
ntum at every stage of the work order lifecycle.



This is a high-volume, fast-paced role where responsiveness and consistency in moving work forward are critical to success. In addition to maintaining workflow, this role supports multi-step coordination by tracking progress, identifying next steps, and helping ensure work is completed efficiently from assignment through completion.



This role is also responsible for building and managing strong working relationships with assigned subcontractors, ensuring alignment with expectations, consistent communication, and reliable performance.



Specific knowledge of a trade or trade-related work—such as plumbing, water remediation, carpentry, or HVAC—is preferred but not required. A strong desire to learn and understand the inner workings of these trades is essential for success in this role.





KEY RESPONSIBILITIES 



Work Order Flow & Execution

 Manage the daily movement of all assigned work orders within your trades 

 Review and take action on new work orders immediately 

 Ensure timely assignment and scheduling of all jobs 

 Actively monitor progress and prevent delays or stalled work 

 Maintain a high level of daily engagement across all active jobs 



Subcontractor Coordination & Relationship Management

 Serve as the primary point of contact for assigned subcontractors 

 Coordinate scheduling between subcontractors and tenants 

 Build and maintain strong working relationships with subcontractors 

 Reinforce expectations around timelines, communication, and documentation 

 Follow up on delays, missed updates, or incomplete work 



Project Support & Multi-Step Coordination

 Track and support jobs that require multiple steps or trades 

 Coordinate next steps to ensure work progresses in the correct sequence 

 Identify when additional work, approvals, or escalation is needed 

 Support overall job progression from assignment through completion 

 Escalate complex or high-risk issues when appropriate 



Communication & Documentation

 Maintain clear, consistent communication with tenants and internal teams 

 Provide timely updates on job status, delays, and next steps 

 Ensure all activity, communication, and updates are documented in ClickUp 

 Keep all parties aligned and informed throughout the lifecycle of the job 



Quality & Completion Oversight

 Confirm work is completed and properly documented before closure 

 Ensure required photos, notes, and details are submitted 

 Identify and flag any issues related to quality or incomplete work 

 Support accurate and clean job completion for invoicing readiness 





REQUIRED SKILLS AND QUALIFICATIONS



 Strong organizational skills with the ability to manage multiple priorities at once 

 High sense of urgency and consistent follow-through 

 Clear and professional communication skills 

 Ability to work in a fast-paced, high-volume environment 

 Strong problem-solving skills and ability to keep work moving forward 

 Calm under pressure, with a natural drive to keep things moving smoothly

 Experience in maintenance coordination, dispatching, or project coordination required



What Success Looks Like

 Maintain a 24-hour active job touch rate of 95%+ across all assigned work orders 

 Ensure new work orders are assigned or scheduled within the same business day

 Maintain zero stalled jobs without documented follow-up or next action 

 Ensure subcontractor scheduling occurs within 24 hours of assignment

 Build and maintain strong subcontractor relationships that support responsiveness and performance

 Keep all communication and updates fully documented in ClickUp





HOW TO APPLY 



Please send a copy of your resume along with the the following:

1. Self-Introduction Video: Please record a short video (1-2 minutes) introducing yourself, highlighting your relevant experience, and answering the following question: What unique strengths do you feel you would bring to this role?



2. Typing Test Result: Please complete a typing test and share the results with us. You can use any online typing test platform of your choice.



3. Internet Speed Test Result: Please perform an internet speed test and provide the results. You can use websites like speedtest.net for this purpose.



We look forward to getting to know you better and discussing how you can contribute to our team.

title: Video and Content Editor
description:
About Funktion Active



Funktion Active is a premium female activewear and athleisure brand where function meets fashion. We create pieces for women who want performance, style, confidence, and versatility in one. Our customer wants apparel that works hard, looks sharp, and feels good in real life — from training sessions to coffee runs to everything in between.



Our brand is built on a few non-negotiables:



Fun — We embrace the grind, support each other, celebrate wins, and bring good energy to the work

Fast & Focused — We move quickly, prioritise what matters, and keep things clean and effective

Funky — We bring creativity, individuality, and personality — always with purpose

Follow-through — We do what we say, communicate clearly, and take ownership

First Class — We care about standards, details, and delivering polished, commercially strong work



Everything we put into the world needs to reflect that — especially our content.



Role Overview



We are looking for a Video and Content Editor who can take large volumes of raw footage and turn it into polished, high-performing, on-brand content for social media, paid ads, and campaigns.



This is not a basic editing role.



We need someone who can shape content that feels sharp, current, clean, and commercially effective — with a strong focus on scroll-stopping, high-performing short-form content.



This role also plays a key part in building and maintaining a strong content reserve, ensuring the brand stays ahead rather than constantly reacting.



Scroll-Stopping & Viral Content Standard



Content should not only look good — it must perform.



We are creating content for fast-moving social environments where attention is earned, not given. Every piece should be designed to stop the scroll within the first few seconds and hold attention through to the end.



This means:



Strong hooks within the first 1–3 seconds

Clean, engaging visual movement

Edits that feel native to the platform

Content that feels current and relevant



We are not chasing trends blindly, but we are also not ignoring them. The expectation is to understand what is working and apply it in a way that still feels on-brand and elevated.



The goal is to consistently produce content that is:



Highly watchable

Easy to consume

Engaging enough to be saved, shared, or replayed

Capable of performing both organically and in paid ads



Good content is not enough. It needs to compete.



Key Responsibilities

Edit raw footage into polished short-form and campaign-ready content

Identify the strongest clips, moments, and creative opportunities from large footage sets

Create clean, engaging edits with strong pacing and flow

Apply colour correction and ensure accurate product colour representation

Format content correctly for Instagram, paid ads, website, and other platforms

Add captions, text overlays, and simple motion elements where needed

Prepare final exports in a clean, organised format for the team

Maintain strong file organisation and naming conventions

Support building and managing a structured, searchable content library

Flag strong unused footage and suggest new content opportunities

Work through large footage banks consistently to build a content reserve

Deliver work that is close to final with minimal revisions required

Identify and prioritise content with strong potential to perform as scroll-stopping social content

Content Focus

Instagram Reels (primary focus)

Gym, fitness, and training content

Paid ad creatives (Meta)

TikTok-style edits

Website and campaign videos

UGC-style and behind-the-scenes content

Product and founder-led content

Expected Output

Up to 150 unique edited pieces per month

Most edits are short-form (~15 seconds)

Work through a 20+ hour footage bank consistently

Maintain a content reserve at least 4 weeks ahead

Creative & Brand Standards



Content should feel:



Fun, engaging, and on-brand

Clean, polished, and premium

Current but not cheap or overdone

Authentic while still elevated



Avoid:



Cheap-looking edits

Overused templates

Cheesy transitions or effects

Sloppy pacing

Visual inconsistency



Reference brands: CSB, Lilo, Hona Active



Workflow & Communication

Work within Google Drive systems for files, briefs, and feedback

Follow provided direction for copy, music, and creative intent

Work independently while staying aligned with priorities and timelines



Communication expectations:



Daily check-in

End-of-day update

Weekly meeting



Daily updates should include:



Completed work

Work in progress

Items awaiting review

Blockers

Next priorities



We value clear, proactive communication — no guessing, no disappearing.



Revisions & Quality Expectations

Most content should require 1–2 revisions max

First drafts should already be close to final

Work should be clean, accurate, and ready to use

What Success Looks Like

Content delivered quickly with minimal hand holding

Consistent editing quality across all outputs

Strong attention to detail and product accuracy

Initiative and problem-solving without constant direction

Content bank stays ahead (minimum 4 weeks)

Files and assets are organised and easy to use

Communication is reliable and proactive

Ideal Candidate

Mid-level to senior editor

Strong experience with short-form and social-first content

Strong eye for pacing, visuals, and content flow

Organised and comfortable managing large volumes of footage

Strong written English and communication

Experience in fitness, activewear, or ecommerce is a plus

Experience with both organic and paid content is a plus

Fast, reliable, detail-oriented, and accountable

Not a Fit If You

Are slow, disorganised, or inconsistent

Need constant direction for simple decisions

Struggle with volume or deadlines

Produce generic, low-quality, or template-heavy work

Miss details or create workflow chaos

Role Structure

Full-time or contract (depending on candidate)

~40 hours per week

Long-term opportunity with growth potential

Confidentiality



You will have access to unreleased campaigns and internal content. Professionalism and discretion are required.



Final Note



We are looking for someone who gives a damn.



Someone fast, sharp, organised, and proud of the work they put out.

Someone who can take raw footage and turn it into content that actually looks like a brand.



Someone who understands:



Fun doesn’t mean messy

Premium doesn’t mean boring

Speed doesn’t mean sloppy



If that sounds like you, we’d love to hear from you.

BE FUNKY!

title: Framer Designer Needed
description:
Framer Designer Needed — Replicate Websites Pixel-Perfect (Ongoing Work)



Overview



We run a website design service and need a skilled Framer designer to join us on an ongoing basis.



Your job is simple: we send you a reference website URL via Vercel and a brief, you recreate it in Framer as close to pixel-perfect as possible. We handle everything else — the client, the tech, the deployment. All you need to do is open the URL, study it, and rebuild it beautifully in Framer.



This is not a creative role. You are not designing from scratch. You are replicating — and replicating well.



---



What Every Brief Looks Like



Every task we give you will follow the same format:



```

Reference URL: [the live website to replicate via Vercel]

Client: [client name]

Business: [what they do]

Branding: [colors / fonts / logo if available]

Copy: [specific text to use, or "match reference"]

```



You open the URL. You study it. You rebuild it in Framer. You send us the staging link and screenshots. That's the job.  There is no new work for you to do, this is a copy and paste job but you must have experience in framer to apply.



---



What We Expect From You



**Pixel-perfect replication**

Colors, fonts, spacing, layout, border radius, shadows — everything should match the reference as closely as Framer allows. We will do a side-by-side comparison. Approximations are not acceptable.



**Mobile responsiveness**

Every site must look correct at 390px (iPhone). You will screenshot every section at mobile and compare it against the reference before submitting. Do not submit without completing mobile QA.



**Clean Framer structure**

Use named Color Styles and Text Styles — no hardcoded inline values. Components should be reusable and the project should be easy for us to edit after handoff.



**Interactions and animations**

If the reference site has scroll animations, sticky navbars, hover states, or component interactions — replicate them. If something cannot be built natively in Framer, use a Marketplace component or a custom Code Component.



**No placeholder images**

If images are not provided in the brief, flag them with the correct dimensions and wait for assets. Do not publish with blank placeholders.



**Fast turnaround**

Standard sites (5–8 sections) should be completed within 24 hours of receiving the brief. We will communicate if timelines differ.



---



## Your Deliverables Per Project



When you submit, we need:



1. Live Framer staging URL

2. Screenshots of every section at desktop (1440px) and mobile (390px)

3. Side-by-side comparisons of your build vs the reference for each section

4. A short written note on anything you couldn't replicate exactly and why



---



You're a Good Fit If You



- Have strong hands-on experience building in Framer (not just designing — actually publishing sites)

- Can replicate layouts accurately without needing creative direction

- Understand Framer's breakpoint system, Stacks, Color Styles, Text Styles, and Code Components

- Are detail-obsessed — you notice when a padding value is 4px off

- Communicate clearly and flag issues early rather than submitting something half-done

- Can work independently from a brief with minimal back and forth



---



You're Not a Good Fit If You



- Are primarily a Figma or Webflow designer who's dabbled in Framer

- Need significant creative direction or hand-holding per project

- Are not comfortable working from a URL as a reference with no design file provided

- Cannot deliver mobile-responsive builds consistently



---

To Apply



Send us:



1. **Your Framer portfolio** — live URLs only, no Figma files or screenshots

2. **One example** of a site you replicated or closely matched to an existing design

3. **Your rate** — per project or hourly, whichever you prefer

4. **Your availability** and average turnaround time for a standard 6–8 section site



Applications without live Framer URLs will not be considered.

title: Sales Developement Representative
description:
Sales Development Representative (SDR) — US Companies, Remote Philippines

About Us

We run two US-based companies in the sourcing and supply chain space. One serves the CBD industry with hardware and packaging. The other serves supplement and nutraceutical brands with full-service sourcing, packaging, and logistics. Both companies source factory direct from China with American partners on the ground ensuring quality — and we pass the savings (20%+) to our customers.

We’re growing fast and need a sharp, self-motivated SDR to help us fill our VP of Sales’ calendar with qualified meetings. You’ll be the engine that drives new business for both companies.

The Role

You will own outreach for two sales programs running simultaneously. Your job is to generate interest and book meetings — not to close deals. We have a VP of Sales who handles closing. You fill his calendar.

You’ll be supported by AI-powered tools that handle
 personalization, sending, and deliverability automatically. Your focus is on the high-value work that AI can’t do: phone calls, LinkedIn relationship building, handling replies, overcoming objections, and booking meetings.

What You’ll Do

• Set up and manage our sales tech stack: 
 (lead enrichment), Instantly.ai 
 automation), 
 (contact database), HubSpot CRM, and Zapier (automations)

• Configure DNS records (SPF, DKIM, DMARC) for
 sending domains — we’ll provide a step-by-step guide

• Enrich lead lists using Clay — pull
s, phone numbers, company data, and generate AI-personalized
 openers for each prospect

• Build and manage cold
 campaigns in Instantly — import leads, set up sequences, monitor deliverability

• Make 40-60 cold calls per day to cannabis growers and supplement brand decision makers using provided scripts

• Send 20-30 LinkedIn connection requests and follow-up messages per day

• Triage and respond to
 replies — flag interested leads, handle objections, and book VP of Sales calls within 1 hour of a positive reply

• Book 5-10 VP of Sales meetings per week (this is the #1 metric we care about)

• Maintain HubSpot CRM with accurate pipeline data, call notes, and deal stages

• Send a structured weekly performance report every Friday

Requirements

• Excellent spoken and written English — you’ll be calling US-based business owners and
ing C-level executives

• Experience with cold calling and/or cold
 outreach (B2B sales development preferred)

• Comfortable setting up and learning new software tools quickly (Clay, Instantly, Apollo, HubSpot, Zapier)

• Basic technical skills — able to follow a step-by-step guide to configure DNS records and connect tools via API/Zapier

• Self-motivated and organized — you’ll work independently with a weekly check-in, not daily supervision

• Available to work US business hours (Eastern or Pacific time zone — evening/night shift Philippines time)

• Reliable internet connection and a quiet space for phone calls

Nice to Have (Not Required)

• Experience with 
, Instantly.ai, 
, or similar sales automation tools

• Familiarity with the supplement or CPG/packaging industries

• Experience setting up
 infrastructure (domains, DNS, warm-up)

• Previous experience booking meetings for a US-based sales team

Compensation & Details

• Pay: $
USD/month (based on experience), paid bi-weekly

• Performance bonus: $25–$50 per qualified VP of Sales meeting booked (on top of base)

• Schedule: Full-time, 40 hours/week, US business hours (evening shift PH)

• Location: Remote — Philippines only

• Start date: As soon as possible

• Long-term position — this is not a short-term project. We want someone who will grow with us.

What We Provide

• Complete outreach playbooks with scripts,
 templates, objection handling, and call scripts — you won’t be guessing what to say

• Pre-built lead lists (5,200+ growers + 40 supplement brands) ready to work

• AI-powered tools that handle
 personalization and sending — you focus on calls and relationships

• Step-by-step setup guides for every tool (Clay, Instantly, DNS, Zapier, HubSpot)

• A clear KPI framework so you always know exactly what success looks like

• Direct access to leadership — small company, no bureaucracy

How to Apply

Send a message with:

• A brief introduction (2-3 sentences about your background)

• Your experience with cold calling, cold
, or B2B sales development

• Any experience with sales tools (CRM,
 automation, lead databases)

• Your English proficiency (spoken and written) — we may ask for a short voice recording

• Your expected monthly salary in USD

Please include the word “TCI PACKAGING Applicant” in the subject of your
ed response.  Only applications with this
 subject sent to the below
 addresses will be assessed.





We review applications within 48 hours and aim to have the right person started within 1-2 weeks.
